title: The day I forgot something important
summary: Narrate a day when you forgot something and describe what happened.
topic: The day I forgot something important
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a day when you forgot something important. What did you forget? When did you realise? What happened because of it? Use past simple and past continuous. Include when, because, and although.
hintStarters: One morning I was in such a hurry that I forgot…
I didn't realise until I was already…
Although I tried to fix the problem, I…

hintVocab.meaningVi: bù đắp lại cho ai đó
bị phân tâm, mất tập trung

sampleText: Last March I forgot a very important meeting at work. I was distracted the whole morning because I was thinking about a problem at home. I made coffee, I answered some emails, and I was sitting at my desk doing nothing important when my manager sent me a message. She asked where I was. I looked at my calendar and I felt my stomach drop. The meeting had started fifteen minutes earlier. I rushed to the meeting room, knocked on the door, and walked in. Everyone turned to look at me. I sat down quickly and apologised. My manager said it was fine, but her face said something different. Although the meeting continued without any big problem, I felt embarrassed for the rest of the day. After work, I apologised again and explained that I had been very distracted. My manager was understanding about it. She told me to use a reminder app so it would not happen again. I downloaded one that evening. Since then, I have not missed a single meeting. I still think about that day when I see a reminder pop up on my phone. It was not a great moment, but I learned from it.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: bị phân tâm cả buổi sáng
tôi cảm thấy ruột gan như sụt xuống
đã bắt đầu từ mười lăm phút trước
vội vã chạy đến phòng họp

sampleAnnotations.phrase: everyone turned to look at me
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: mọi người quay lại nhìn tôi
ngồi xuống nhanh và xin lỗi
nét mặt của cô ấy nói lên điều gì đó khác

sampleAnnotations.phrase: felt embarrassed for the rest of the day
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cảm thấy xấu hổ suốt phần còn lại của ngày
không bỏ lỡ một cuộc họp nào
tôi học được từ điều đó

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous for ongoing background states
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng past continuous để mô tả những gì đang xảy ra liên tục như một nền bối cảnh: 'I was distracted the whole morning', 'I was sitting at my desk'. Những hành động cụ thể (past simple) xảy ra trong khoảng thời gian đó.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use past continuous for ongoing background states: 'I was distracted the whole morning', 'I was sitting at my desk'. Specific completed actions (past simple) happen within that time frame.

sampleTranslationVi: Tháng Ba năm ngoái tôi quên một cuộc họp rất quan trọng ở công ty. Tôi bị phân tâm cả buổi sáng vì đang lo nghĩ về một vấn đề ở nhà. Tôi pha cà phê, trả lời một số email, và đang ngồi ở bàn làm việc không làm gì quan trọng thì quản lý tôi nhắn tin. Cô hỏi tôi đang ở đâu. Tôi nhìn vào lịch và tôi cảm thấy ruột gan như sụt xuống. Cuộc họp đã bắt đầu từ mười lăm phút trước. Tôi vội vã chạy đến phòng họp, gõ cửa và bước vào. Mọi người quay lại nhìn tôi. Tôi ngồi xuống nhanh và xin lỗi. Quản lý nói không sao, nhưng nét mặt của cô nói lên điều gì đó khác. Dù cuộc họp tiếp tục mà không có vấn đề lớn, tôi vẫn cảm thấy xấu hổ suốt phần còn lại của ngày. Sau giờ làm, tôi xin lỗi thêm lần nữa và giải thích rằng tôi đã rất phân tâm. Quản lý tôi thông cảm về điều đó. Cô bảo tôi dùng ứng dụng nhắc nhở để không xảy ra lần nữa. Tối đó tôi tải về. Từ đó đến nay, tôi không bỏ lỡ một cuộc họp nào. Tôi vẫn nghĩ đến ngày đó mỗi khi thấy thông báo nhắc nhở hiện ra trên điện thoại. Đó không phải khoảnh khắc hay ho, nhưng tôi đã học được từ nó.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses past continuous correctly for a background action?
options: I was sitting at my desk when my manager sent me a message.
I sat at my desk when my manager was sending me a message.
I was sit at my desk when my manager sent me a message.
I sitting at my desk when my manager sent me a message.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous 'was sitting' shows the background action; past simple 'sent' shows the interrupting event.
mcQuestions.hint: Past continuous = was/were + -ing for the background.

options: to suddenly understand or become aware of something
to remember something from the past
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Realise' means to suddenly become aware of something.
mcQuestions.hint: Which verb means to suddenly become aware of a fact?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple: 'She asked where I ___.'
mcQuestions.explanation: In reported speech after a past verb, 'was' (past of 'am') is correct.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'asked' (past), the reported verb shifts to the past.

options: doing something very quickly because of time pressure
doing something for the first time
mcQuestions.explanation: 'In a hurry' means rushing because you don't have much time.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrase describes rushing due to lack of time?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word means feeling embarrassed about something you did?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Embarrassed' means feeling self-conscious or ashamed about something.
mcQuestions.hint: Which adjective means feeling ashamed or awkward?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct use of 'although': '___ the meeting continued, I felt embarrassed.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Although' introduces a contrast: the meeting was fine, but you still felt bad.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word shows something unexpected happened despite the situation?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means 'to go back to a place you left'?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Turn back' means to reverse direction and go back where you came from.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrase means reversing course to return somewhere?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word means unable to focus because of other thoughts?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Distracted' means unable to focus because your attention is elsewhere.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'She told me to use a reminder app so it ___ not happen again.'
mcQuestions.explanation: After 'told me', use 'would' for reported future speech.
mcQuestions.hint: Use 'would' for reported future in past context.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'miss' mean in 'I missed the meeting'?
options: to fail to attend or catch something
to think about something with sadness
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Miss' means to fail to attend, catch, or be present for something.
mcQuestions.hint: Which meaning of 'miss' relates to not being present for an event?

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'make it up to someone' mean?
options: to do something good to compensate for a mistake
to invent a story for someone
to arrive on time for someone
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Make it up to someone' means to compensate for a mistake by doing something kind.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrase means 'to compensate for a mistake'?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Do you think forgetting important things is always careless, or can there be other reasons? What do you think is the best way to stay organised and avoid forgetting things?
